question: A particle moves along a straight line such that its velocity v = √s where s is displacement. The acceleration of the particle is:
A ball is dropped from a height h. At the same time another ball is thrown upwards from the ground with a velocity v. They meet at h/2. The value of v is:
The velocity of a particle is v = v₀ + gt + ft². Its position at t=0 is x=0. The displacement after time t=1 is:
A particle moving with constant acceleration covers 10m in the 3rd second and 15m in the 5th second. The acceleration is:

A car accelerates from rest at a constant rate α for some time, after which it decelerates at a constant rate β and comes to rest. If the total time elapsed is T, the maximum velocity acquired by the car is:
The relation between time t and distance x is t = ax² + bx, where a and b are constants. The retardation is (v is velocity):
A bullet fired into a fixed target loses half of its velocity after penetrating 3 cm. How much further will it penetrate before coming to rest?
A body starts from rest and moves with uniform acceleration. The ratio of distance covered in nth second to the distance covered in n seconds is:
The displacement of a particle is given by x = (t-2)² where x is in meters and t in seconds. The distance covered by the particle in first 4 seconds is:
A lift is moving up with acceleration a. A person inside the lift throws a ball upwards with velocity u relative to lift. The time of flight is:
A particle moves in a straight line with deceleration proportional to the square of its velocity. If the initial velocity is v₀, the velocity after time t is:
A ball is thrown vertically upwards. Which of the following graphs represent the velocity-time graph of the ball during its flight (ignoring air resistance)?

options.A: A straight line with negative slope
options.C: A straight line with positive slope

question: The ratio of the distances travelled by a freely falling body in the 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th second is:
A body is thrown vertically upwards with velocity u. The distance travelled by it in the fifth and the sixth seconds are equal. The velocity u is (g = 9.8 m/s²):
If velocity of a particle is v = At + Bt², where A and B are constants, then the distance travelled by it between 1s and 2s is:
A stone is dropped from a rising balloon at a height of 76m from the ground. If the balloon is rising with a speed of 9.8 m/s, for how long will the stone take to touch the ground?

Two cars A and B are moving in the same direction with velocities 30 m/s and 20 m/s. When car A is 100m behind B, the driver of A applies brakes and produces a uniform retardation of 2 m/s². The minimum distance between them is:
A particle starts from rest and its acceleration at t=0 is 5 m/s². If its acceleration a = 5 - 2t, the maximum velocity of the particle is:
The displacement-time graph of two particles A and B are straight lines making angles of 30° and 60° with the time axis. The ratio of their velocities vA:vB is:
A man is standing on top of a tower 40m high. He throws a ball vertically upwards with 10 m/s. After how much time will it strike the ground? (g=10)
The velocity-displacement graph of a particle is a circle with center at origin and radius R. The maximum acceleration is:
A particle moves such that its position x = 3t² - 6t. The time when the particle changes its direction of motion is:
The velocity of a particle at any time t is given by v = 2t(3-t) m/s. At what time is the velocity maximum?
A body is moving with variable acceleration a = kt. If the body starts from rest at x=0, its displacement after time t is:

A police jeep is chasing a culprit going on a motorbike. The motorbike crosses a turning at 72 km/h. The jeep follows it at 90 km/h, crossing the turning 10s later. How far from the turning will the jeep catch the culprit?
For a particle moving in a straight line, the v-t graph is shown. The total distance covered is the:
A body travels 200 cm in the first 2s and 220 cm in the next 4s. What will be the velocity at the end of the 7th second?
A tap drips at a rate of 1 drop/sec. The distance between 1st and 2nd drop when the 3rd drop is about to fall is (g=10):
Which of the following speed-time graphs is physically impossible?

options.A: A graph with two values of speed at one time

question: A particle moves with a velocity v(t) = v₀(1 - t/T) for 0 ≤ t ≤ T. The average velocity for this interval is:
